steps.title: Spot the quantity signal
steps.content: The phrase "flood of interesting writing" is the key clue. "Flood" is a metaphor for a large, sudden abundance of something. The blank must name this phenomenon of plentiful literary production that emerged after the Revolution.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
The blank needs a word meaning an abundant flow or surge of literary works. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — outpouring: means a sudden, copious flow of something — directly mirrors "flood" and captures the abundant production of poetry, fiction, and essays. ✓
  • Choice B — overturning: means a reversal or overthrow; it describes changing something, not producing an abundance of it. ✗
  • Choice C — censure: means formal disapproval or condemnation; the passage praises this writing, and censure is unrelated to literary quantity. ✗
  • Choice D — protection: means shielding from harm; it has no connection to the volume or production of literary works. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
